title: California
location: Orange County and San Diego, California
subtitle: An indoor kid (me) reluctantly braves the sun and beaches of the golden state
blurb: I flew out west for work travel and was able to append a trip down to Southern California to spend some time with my parents. My personality (and UV tolerance) is basically the opposite of a beach bum's, but over time I am gradually starting to appreciate the sun and sand of the south coast.

20260502_01.alt: Grassy foreground with a sliver of blue ocean in the background, with a sharp horizon
20260502_01.caption: Crystal Cove State Park

20260502_02.alt: Curvy hills with some desert grasses, and the blue Pacific Ocean in the background
20260502_02.caption: Crystal Cove State Park

20260502_04.alt: Looking out at the rocks and water at Crystal Cove State Park late afternoon, the foreground partially shadowed
20260502_04.caption: Crystal Cove State Park

20260502_05.caption: Crystal Cove State Park
20260502_05.alt: A craggy rock formation with hundreds of erosion lines, the camera is about 10 feet away from the rock

20260502_06.alt: My dad, wearing a baseball cap and standing on a rock at the beach, looking down at the water contemplatively
20260502_06.caption: Contemplative dad

20260502_07.alt: My mom, wearing a large sun visor and standing on wet sand looking out at the water at the beach
20260502_07.caption: Mom observing

20260502_08.alt: Me, a middle aged Korean woman, taking a landscape-oriented selfie at the beach. I'm wearing a seafoam green visor and behind me are some rocks and the ocean

20260502_09.alt: Two older adults walking along the beach

20260502_10.alt: Lifeguard station on the sand at the beach, with a wall of green foliage behind

20260502_11.alt: Cracked, dry rock at the beach, shot from above

20260502_28.alt: Me taking a landscape selfie at the beach. I'm wearing sunglasses and the sky and ocean are impossibly blue.

20260503_12.alt: Two older Korean adults, one holding up a large picture menu, both smiling. There are pictures and Korean names of two dishes on the window behind them: Korean sausage soup and ox bone soup. It has the feel of a cozy Korean restaurant that hasn't changed in decades.
20260503_12.caption: If the Korean soup spot doesn't have this vibe, I don't want it

20260503_13.alt: 반찬 (banchan) and rice in various bowls on a wooden table
20260503_13.caption: If the banchan isn't this fresh, I don't want it!!

20260503_14.alt: A piping hot bowl of 감자탕 (gamja tang, spicy pork bone soup with potato and perilla) in a stone pot

20260503_15.alt: Older Korean man wearing a hat with a California patch holding a cup of Starbucks coffee
20260503_15.caption: My dad wanted to show me Fashion Island where he and my mom go to share a cup of Starbucks and people-watch 😭

20260503_16.alt: A blue street sign that says Avocado (as in Avocado Street), with palm trees in the background. It's all very California

20260503_17.alt: Exterior of Newport Beach Public Library, floor-to-ceiling glass panels with a huge roof overhang providing shade over the patrons sitting at tables outside the entrance, and a perfectly manicured green lawn
20260503_17.caption: You know a public library hates to see me coming. (I love public libraries.)

20260503_18.alt: A dark parking garage, with a rectangle cut out of the ceiling through which tall bamboo grows and sunlight is visible
20260503_18.caption: I like that they planted this bamboo and cut out part of the parking garage to let some natural light in

20260503_19.alt: Tower scopes with a red base at Corona del Mar, looking out at Newport Beach
20260503_19.caption: Corona del Mar

20260503_20.caption: Corona del Mar
20260503_20.alt: A greenish scene near the beach, with palm trees and grass in foreground, and a visible path of stairs down to the sand and water

20260503_21.alt: A man sits on the armrest of a white park bench, looking out over the beach with a pair of binoculars
20260503_21.caption: Peepin'

20260503_24.alt: Overhead view of the beach at Corona del Mar, with foamy whitewater against the sand and dozens of scattered beachgoers

20260503_25.alt: Moody beach with rocks and gray-blue water

20260503_26.alt: Dramatic beach with big sharp rocks and gray-blue water

20260504_22.alt: Manicured lawn with two geese grazing - actually, one grazing, and the other one watching her 6. The (presumably) male has a bandit-like dark coloring over his eye.
20260504_22.caption: I observed this very dramatic looking bird couple on a few of my morning walks. Turns out they are Egyptian geese (and they did not consort with the many Canadian geese in the area)

20260504_27.alt: A distant view of a long sandy beach with blue-green water
20260504_27.caption: Torrey Pines State Beach

20260504_29.caption: Torrey Pines State Beach
20260504_29.alt: Craggy rocks with many layers of time baked in

20260504_30.alt: A view of the beach between multiple rock formations
20260504_30.caption: Hiking at Torrey Pines State Beach - beach views for much of the walk. Reminded me of the Lanikai Pillbox hike in Oahu.

20260504_31.alt: Sandy hiking trail between mounds of desert flora, with the blue ocean in the near distance

20260504_32.alt: Two older adults in brightly colored hiking gear, looking fit and energized

20260504_33.alt: Desert hills in a sort of V shape, with the Pacific Ocean visible in the cup of the V

20260504_34.alt: A Torrey pine leaning into the foreground, with beautiful blue-green water of the Pacific in the background
20260504_34.caption: She's *thee* Torrey pine!

20260505_23.alt: An artificial green-grey pond with trees and a manicured lawn in the distance
20260505_23.caption: Nice outdoor scene near my parents' place

20260505_35.alt: Desert flowers and mountains in front of a blue sky
20260505_35.caption: View from near the top at Aliso and Wood Canyons Wilderness Park
